title: niebuhria apetala
intro: Niebuhria apetala, also known as the "Buddha's hand" or "fingered citron," is a rare and unique species of citrus plant, characterized by its finger-like appendages and lack of a central fruit. The medicinal properties of Niebuhria apetala include its high concentration of limonene, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion. Herbal preparations of Niebuhria apetala can be made into teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
